meaning: v.发出叫声,诉苦,抗议n.叫声,诉苦,抗议,故障
examples: This file contains information that the Squawk VM will use to run the application.
The bird gave a startled squawk.
A mallard suddenly took wing, rising steeply into the air with an angry squawk.
It's easy to see why Rodgers would squawk about the proposal to expense options.
After the usual screech and squawk sounds ordinarily associated with a modem connection, e-mail is downloaded and received.
